$AMZN LEO has already gone from 27 to 29 satellites per Atlas V launch through hardware optimizations.

That means the network can scale faster without needing more launches.

$TSLA may ultimately be remembered as the company whose autonomy lead unlocked the robotics revolution

If Tesla truly solves autonomy then the real opportunity expands into labor, entirely new end markets and a new industrial frontier for humanity.

$AMZN AWS has become one of the largest recurring revenue engines in the world. 

AI is now giving that engine a new leg of growth.

If the market is going to crowd into public space proxies ahead of SpaceX then I think $RKLB & $ASTS are two of the best places for that capital to land. 

Rocket Lab gives you a real space infrastructure platform across launch, spacecraft, defense & Neutron upside while AST is going after one of the most commercially valuable use cases in the category (space-based cellular broadband).

A $2T SpaceX would instantly become the world’s sixth-largest public company: 

1. $NVDA ~$4.3T
2. $AAPL ~$3.8T
3. $GOOGL ~$3.6T 
4. $MSFT ~$2.8T 
5. $AMZN ~$2.3T 
6. SpaceX ($2T)*

Another example of the AI economy may be banks rebuilt from the inside by people using AI to speed up decisions & automate workflows.

The scarce asset will be the people who actually know how to turn AI into real operating change which is why entirely new roles will likely be created in this next era.

Jim Simons believed in hiring smart people, giving them freedom & creating an environment where everyone could talk to each other

In the AI era that becomes even more relevant because breakthrough AI comes from pairing talent density with the best compute, data & infrastructure

Perplexity Computer can now help users prepare federal tax returns with its new “Navigate my taxes” feature.

Another sign AI is moving into tax prep which is a category long dominated by incumbents like $INTU.

Cathie Wood says Bitcoin’s days of the typical 85% crash are over, calling it a proven technology, a proven monetary system and a new asset class.

Her point is that $BTC is maturing from a speculative trade into a more established part of the financial system.

$GOOGL Cloud operating margin expansion over the past few years has been nuts to watch.

That’s what happens when you pair custom silicon with one of the largest global data center footprints then layer in distribution across Workspace, Search & YouTube plus first-party models like Gemini.

MARCH U.S. JOBS REPORT

• Nonfarm Payrolls +178K vs Est. +65K
• Unemployment Rate 4.3% vs Est. 4.4%

The labor market did not crack here.

🚨 Jamie Dimon explains why people are leaving New York

"Our head count in Manhattan when I got to JPMorgan was 35,000 and now is 26,000. Our head count in Texas started at 11,000, now it's 33,000. That's what happens."  

Jamie Dimon on why companies are leaving New York:  

"Highest individual taxes, highest estate taxes, highest corporate taxes, anti-business sentiment."  

"When I grew up as a kid in New York City, there were 120 of the Fortune 500 headquarters there. In the 1970s, 60 of the 120 left, including Exxon, GE, IBM, Union Carbide. They're all going to Texas."

Markets take the stairs up and the elevator down."

This chart proves it beyond any doubt.

S&P 500. Every bull and bear market since 1949.

Bull markets:
→ Average 5.3 years
→ Average +254%
→ Longest: 12.3 years, +841%

Bear markets:
→ Average 1.0 years
→ Average −31%
→ Worst: 1.4 years, −55%

The asymmetry is breathtaking.

You spend years building wealth on the stairs.
Then the elevator arrives and takes a chunk back in months.

But here's what the chart really shows.

The blue always came back.
Every single time.
And always went higher than before.

The bear markets look terrifying in the moment.
On a 75-year chart, they look like speed bumps.

The stairs always win.
Time is the only ticket you need.
